items.correct: An arrangement where a franchisor grants a franchisee the right to use their brand, products and systems in exchange for fees and a share of revenue.
items.misconception: A franchise is when a business owns multiple branches itself.
items.random_wrong: A franchise is when employees buy shares in their company.
items.why: Owned chains are not franchises. A franchise involves separate ownership — McDonald's restaurants are mostly franchised (owned by individual operators), not owned by McDonald's Corporation.

items.q: What are the four Ps of the marketing mix?
items.correct: Product, Price, Place (distribution), and Promotion — the controllable variables a business uses to satisfy customers and achieve objectives.
items.misconception: The four Ps are Product, Price, Profit, and People.
items.random_wrong: The four Ps are Planning, Producing, Promoting, and Profit.
items.why: Students often substitute 'profit' or 'people' into the four Ps. The classic McCarthy framework is Product, Price, Place and Promotion — extended models add others but those four are foundational.

items.correct: The external macro-environment: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal and Environmental factors affecting a business.
items.misconception: PESTLE analyses the internal strengths and weaknesses of a business.
items.random_wrong: PESTLE stands for Profit, Earnings, Sales, Trade, Loans, Equity.
items.why: Students confuse PESTLE (external) with SWOT (internal + external). PESTLE is about the wider environment a business cannot control; SWOT mixes internal and external.

items.q: A leader who sets goals then leaves the team alone uses which style?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Laissez-faire. “Autocratic” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Continuum. “Pyramid” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Tannenbaum & Schmidt publish their continuum?
items.why: Exact answer: 1958. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
